name: Project Manager
description: Generic project-manager agent: instantiates a typed project template into the configured PM work store and ticks it to completion. Thin tick-shell: the flow carries the deterministic phase sequencing (instantiate -> tick -> report); every state decision is made by the host primitives (instantiateProject, the readyItems validator, dispatchProjectWorker), never by prose.

projectPrimitiveTools.description: The host tool surface this tick-shell drives. Each tool is a run-token-authenticated MCP tool the host exposes to the PM seat's own run; the host binds every hostDerived operand server-side (auth-derived org, the persisted instance, the live project lease, the calling tick run) and NEVER accepts them from the model. agentSupplied lists are the COMPLETE set the model may pass - pinned by this repo's tick-shell contract tests against the host primitive input contracts (cinatra src/lib/project-instantiation.ts + src/lib/project-dispatch.ts).

project_instantiate.primitive: instantiateProject + deterministic materialization (materializeProjectTemplate find-or-create into the persisted provider's PmWorkStore; anchorDate is the MaterializeOptions.anchorDate operand - instantiateProject itself takes no date)

project_tick_context.primitive: readyItems (sdk-extensions project-template-contract) over the persisted instance's live PM work items

instantiate.name: Instantiate the project (host primitive, sticky fail-closed)

data.system: You are the Project Manager agent in the INSTANTIATE phase. Follow the SKILL.md INSTANTIATE recipe exactly and return a single JSON object as specified there - nothing else.

cinatra.description: Thin shell over the host instantiateProject primitive: PM-seat kind gate, template authority (installed finalized payload, never caller bytes), once-at-instantiation provider selection (configured wins; auto iff exactly one; fail-closed on none/several), sticky-binding idempotency/INSTANCE_DRIFT. The model passes ONLY the agentSupplied arguments; the host derives orgId and the PM seat from the authenticated run.

tick.name: Tick: ready set -> dispatch workers, surface human work

data.system: You are the Project Manager agent in the TICK phase. Follow the SKILL.md TICK recipe exactly and return a single JSON object as specified there - nothing else.
data.user:
PHASE: TICK

Instantiation phase result (JSON): {{ instantiation }}

Tick the project:
1. Call project_tick_context with projectRef: {{ project_ref }} and asOf: {{ as_of }} (omit asOf when blank; the host defaults it to today). The host returns the persisted instance, every cinatra-managed work item in the project scope, and the machine-computed ready set.
2. For EACH ready item whose template task binds a worker role, call project_dispatch_worker with exactly: projectRef, pick (the item's naturalKey), role (the template task's role - never a package name), asOf, actionVersion (0 unless the SKILL.md recovery rule says otherwise), runInput (per the SKILL.md runInput rule).
3. NEVER dispatch human or approval items; report them as waiting-on-human instead.

Then return the phase-result JSON per the SKILL.md TICK recipe.

cinatra.description: Thin shell over the host tick primitives. Readiness is the deterministic sdk-extensions readyItems predicate computed HOST-side (deps done + pickable status + unclaimed + start-date reached); the dispatch primitive re-validates the pick, the role binding, the worker allowlist, the lease, and the ledger idempotency before any child run exists. The model proposes picks from the host-computed ready set only; a hallucinated pick is rejected deterministically.
